description: Feature implementation happens on a branch off the active integration branch and merges back to it. The integration branch is whatever the current arc is based on — context-jit-spec-v2 now, main or a dev branch later; the rule is general, not tied to a specific branch name. Split: CODE goes on the feature branch (packages/**; dist is gitignored, so rebuild on switch). The .context SUBSTRATE stays single-writer on the integration branch — file the gap/task/criteria there BEFORE branching, and run the closure cascade (verification + complete-task + close-gap) there AFTER the code merges and re-verifies, never on the feature branch — so JSON-array block merges never conflict and a rejected feature simply leaves its task planned. Accept: checkout the integration branch, merge the feature branch, npm run build, then file the closure. Reject: checkout the integration branch and delete the feature branch — no reverts, no dirty state. Establish a clean tree before branching; implement via a foreground coding subagent. Pure substrate/planning work (filing gaps/decisions/research) is done directly on the integration branch and needs no feature branch.
